observations.recommendation: 4. The Accounting Office to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary details for each class of PPE reported in the FS, including additions or disposals, its useful lives, and other necessary information to make the FS more comprehensible.
9. Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete LRN Property Card for all roads and its components using the prescribed template; and
10. Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
11. Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for each transfer and for future transfers from NGAs, whether or not maintained in separate bank accounts;
12. Adjustments of necessary corresponding accounts such as liabilities, etc by the Accounting Office; and
13. The immediate submission to the Auditor’s Office of status report pertaining to the physical and financial accomplishment of the projects.

14. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, necessary supporting schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

19. We also recommended that the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM Officer, be supported with documentation of the programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but not limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan, copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization of QRF and Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and photographs of projects/programs/activities undertaken during the month of report.
22. The Accounting/Treasurer’s Office to submit the Bank Statements, Bank Reconciliation Statements and Deposit Slips to substantiate the claim of creation of a separate SEF account.
24. Prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month, attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.
25. The Municipal Treasurer shall observe the stamping of “PAID”, with the prescribed size of 2’’ x 3’’, on all the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents after payment is made.

26. The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims.
27. The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s Office are stamped “PAID”.
30. Mandate the Accountant to religiously conform to the required disclosure of PPE in the financial statements pursuant to IPSAS 17.
41. Prepare a schedule of cancelled/voided checks, if any, at the end of the month, and attach the same to the RCD to be submitted to the Auditor.
46. Hence, we recommended that Honorable Mayor supervise the immediate submission of duly approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan 2019-2029; and
47. Direct the Board, if any, to review the same every two years or as the need arises for purposes of continuity and harmonizing the LGU’s projects/programs with the mandate of RA 9003.

1.10 Therefore, we recommended and Management agreed to mandate that:
a) The pictures of the supplies be taken upon its acceptance and
inspection to affirm its delivery and receipt, and the same be
attached as a supporting document for payment thereof;
b) The recording of all purchased supplies and materials be coursed
through its respective inventory accounts upon acceptance and
inspection thereof;
c) The issuance of all supplies and materials be supported with RIS;
d) The (weekly) SSMI be prepared by the Municipal GSO based
from the RISs;
e) A JEV be prepared by the Municipal Accountant to record
issued/expensed supplies based on the SSMI;
f) The Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards be completely
maintained by the Municipal GSO and the Municipal
Accountant, respectively, by stock number, and the same be
regularly reconciled; and
g) The semestral RPCI be prepared based on the results of the
thorough physical count of inventories and thereupon submitted
to the Auditor’s Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of
each year.

2.8 Hence, we recommended and Management concurred that the Local
Chief Executive mandate:
a) The evaluation of composition of the Inventory Committee at the
beginning of the year, if warranted, replace and/or augment the
members with active and trained personnel who can conduct the
physical count effectively;
b) The Inventory Committee to prepare a PIP, to be approved by the
LCE, which shall guide them in conducting and completing the
annual physical count of all PPEs;
c) The Inventory Committee to assign prescribed unique property
numbers on existing PPEs during the physical count and the
Municipal GSO to do the same on newly procured properties and
equipment;
d) The Inventory Committee to identify the actual condition of the
properties counted and include its status in the report;
e) The Inventory committee to submit the RPCPPE within the
deadline set by the Commission to the Auditor’s Office not later
than January 31 of each year;
f) The complete keeping, maintenance and regular reconciliation of
the property cards, and the PPELC with the results of the physical
count; and
g) Lastly, pending the approval of the supplemental guidelines for
COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 on the increase in
capitalization threshold from 15,000.00 to 50,000.00 of tangible
items, we recommended that all existing equipment, especially
those below ₱50,000.00, be identified, immediately assigned with
prescribed unique property numbers, and listed in a schedule, in
preparation for reclassification once the supplemental guidelines
is approved.

3.9 Therefore, we reiterated our prior year recommendations and
Management agreed to enjoin the department heads’ compliance with
the Circular and direct:
a) The Inventory Committee to conduct an extensive inventory of the
road network system under the LGU’s jurisdiction and prepare a
report thereon in accordance with templates provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-008;
b) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
c) The General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a local road
inventory, road map and keep a complete and updated Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
d) The Municipal Accountant to prepare/adjust the Journal Entry
Voucher, as necessary, to record the beginning/adjusted balance
of the local road network and its components in the general ledger
and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and
subsidiary records for all roads and road components based on
the result of the physical count;
e) The Municipal Accountant to prepare a monthly lapsing schedule
for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year; and
f) The submission of the following to the Auditor’s Office at the end
of the accounting period, preferably not later than January 31 of
each year:
i. Report on Local Road Network by the GSO;
ii. Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System
by the Inventory Committee; and
iii. Disclosure of Local Road Network System in the Notes to
Financial Statements by the Accounting Office.

4.8 Thus, we reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive compel the:
a) LDRRMO to attach the required documents needed to support
the release of LDRRMF funds and to substantiate the monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds with
documentation such as photos of the programs/projects/and
activities undertaken;
b) The Inventory Committee to include in the physical count and in
the RPCPPE the composition of the DRRM equipment;
c) The Accounting Office to prepare and maintain the subisidiary
ledgers and reconcile the same against the RPCPPE; and
d) The on-time submission of the MONTHLY Report of Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM to the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th
day of the ensuing month.

5.11 Thus, we reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive direct:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule showing all the fund
transfers received by the Management as of date, and another
schedule showing the breakdown of the CIP-Infrastructure
account;
b) The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry
Vouchers (JEVs) to establish the trust liability account based on
the submitted schedule to correct the ledgers;
c) The Municipal Accountant to keep separate subsidiary records
for each existing fund transfer and for future fund transfers from
sources agencies;
d) The Municipal Accountant to submit the RCIs and RODs related
to the fund transfers, together with its vouchers and supporting
documents to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days from the
end of the month;
e) The MPDO to submit to the Auditor’s Office the list of all on-
going and to be implemented PPAs, properly categorized whether
under the General or under the Trust Fund, at the beginning of
the year; and
f) The Municipal Engineer and/or the MPDO to include in the
monthly monitoring report of PPAs the physical and financial
status of projects funded through fund transfers.

6.5 Therefore, we recommended and Management concurred that the
Local Chief Executive direct:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to follow-up and promptly request from
the AGDB the release of bank statements of all accounts within
five (5) days at the end of the month; and
b) Thereupon, furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant who
shall prepare and submit the BRS, which shall be submitted to the
Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month.
The Accountant should also see to it that prior to submission, the
BRS, for each account under each fund, are all supported with
bank statements.

7.4 Thus, we recommended and Management concurred that the Local
Chief Executive direct the Treasurer’s Office to submit the copies of
the RPT official receipts together with the RCDs and adopt the same
for the ensuing years.

8.4 Therefore, we recommended and Management concurred that the
Local Chief Executive mandate:
a) The HRMO to submit a complete listing of all the JOs hired by
the Management, by department, and with information on age
and period of contract;
b) All the JOs to submit their DTRs or certification of services
rendered duly signed by the immediate supervisor,
Accomplishment Reports duly approved and signed by the
immediate supervisor, and copies of contracts on which the
Statements of Duties and Responsibilities to be attached to and as
basis for the preparation of payroll. Otherwise, payments thereof
maybe suspended or disallowed, if warranted.

9.6 Thus, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive:
a) Compel the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the
financial transactions covering the period October to December
2022 to the Auditor’s Office.
b) Henceforth, compel the Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit
all the financial reports to the Municipal Accountant, together
with the DVs and supporting documents, for recording in the
books of accounts; and
c) The Municipal Accountant, after recording the reports in the
books of accounts, submit the same to the Auditor’s Office not
later the 10th day of the following month.

1. The GSO and Accounting Office to trace the date of acquisition of the newly acquired/procured PPE and faithfully compute and record depreciation in the related property cards and ledgers, respectively;
2. The Accounting Office to reassess the useful life of the PPE in accordance with DPWH Department Order No. 176, s. 2015 and NGAS Manual for LGUs;
3. The Accounting Office to take up the necessary corresponding adjustments on affected accounts such as PPE asset, expense, and government equity account; and

5. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an extensive and thorough physical count of the road network system under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare the reports thereon in accordance with templates provided in COA Circular 2015-008
6. Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network based on the result of the physical count
7. Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year
8. Instruct the GSO to religiously maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map
15. Faithfully submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-going PPAs and those to be implemented, with proper details as mentioned in COA Circular 2013-004; and
16. Henceforth, prepare and regularly submit the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPAs and quarterly report on the utilization of 20% Development Fund to the Auditor’s Office.
17. The immediate submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds for the months of October to December 2021

18. On-time preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis, duly noted by the MDRRM Officer, and regular submission thereof to the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
20. The Treasurer’s Office to faithfully deposit the SET collections in a separate depository account to prevent merging of funds with that of the General Fund
21. The Treasurer’s Office to faithfully prepare separate reports of collections for the SET proceeds
23. Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled checks

28. Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the Manual

29. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an extensive annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year; and

31. Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the appropriate ledgers;
32. Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards
33. Direct the two (2) offices to religiously reconcile records with ledgers in a regular basis

34. Direct the Inventory Committee to validate the same by conducting a semestral physical count of inventory by items. Thereafter, prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the audit team not later than July 31, and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

35. Immediately instruct the Accountant to prepare an amortization schedule of the outstanding loan and reconcile the same with the statement of accounts from the servicing bank and update the related accounting ledger to reflect the correct balance

36. Instruct the same to update and reconcile the accounting ledgers with the statement of accounts from GSIS, Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and BIR to reflect correct balances of related liability accounts, and if there are discrepancies found, immediately make the necessary adjustments.
37. Prepare the monthly C/RAAF at the end of each month, complete in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations; and
38. Strictly submit the same to the audit team not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month.
39. Completely attach all duplicate copies of all issued, and cancelled ORs to the RCD;
40. Attach the validated deposit slips to the RCD as proof of deposit of collections
42. Record the transactions on a daily basis and stop the practice of recording transactions based from the bank statements
43. Record the check issuances in a chronological order, including the cancelled ones;
44. Completely report and include in the RCD all collections recorded in the cashbook
45. The Treasurer religiously liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period and in accordance with the rules and regulations provided by the Commission.
48. Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
49. Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment of its constituents.
50. Instruct the keeping of an updated real property tax account register to the concerned officials

51. Direct the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit the updated list of duly certified name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the accountant at the beginning of the year

52. We, therefore, reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Honorable Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.

53. We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

54. We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly the BRS together with the original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in bank account.

55. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.

56.Create and Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and required the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to update their ledger cards and stock cards and make the necessary reconciliation thereof

44)Direct the Board, if any, to review the same every two years or as the need arises for the purposes continuity and harmonizing the LGU’s projects/programs with the mandate of RA 9003.

1.12 Hence, we strongly recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct:
a) The GSO and Accounting Office to trace the date of acquisition
of the newly acquired/procured PPE and faithfully compute and
record depreciation in the related property cards and ledgers,
respectively;
b) The Accounting Office to reassess the useful life of the PPE in
accordance with DPWH Department Order No. 176, s. 2015 and
NGAS Manual for LGUs;
c) The Accounting Office to take up the necessary corresponding
adjustments on affected accounts such as PPE asset, expense, and
government equity account; and
d) The Accounting Office to disclose in the Notes to FS the
necessary details for each class of PPE reported in the FS,
including additions or disposals, its useful lives, and other
necessary information to make the FS more comprehensible.

2.7 Therefore, we strongly recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an
extensive and thorough physical count of the road network
system under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare the reports
thereon in accordance with templates provided in COA Circular
2015-008;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road
network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the GSO to religiously maintain Local Road Inventory
and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete LRN Property
Card for all roads and its components using the prescribed
template; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

3.4 In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended that the
LCE instruct the following:
a) Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for
each transfer and for future transfers from NGAs, whether or not
maintained in separate bank accounts;
b) Adjustments of necessary corresponding accounts such as
liabilities, etc by the Accounting Office; and
c) The immediate submission to the Auditor’s Office of status report
pertaining to the physical and financial accomplishment of the
projects.

4.4 Thus, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor compel the BAC
Secretariat to regularly furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of the
POs, together with its supporting documents, within five (5) days upon
perfection thereof, for evaluation.

5.4 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the
concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-
closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, and necessary supporting
schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the
end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

6.7 In view thereof, we recommended that the LCE instruct the MPDO to:
a) Faithfully submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-
going PPAs and those to be implemented, with proper details as
mentioned in COA Circular 2013-004; and
b) Henceforth, prepare and regularly submit the monthly
monitoring report on the status of PPAs and quarterly report on
the utilization of 20% Development Fund to the Auditor’s Office.

7.3 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
a) The immediate submission of the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds for the months of October to
December 2021;
b) On-time preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of
und LDRRMF by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis, duly
noted by the MDRRM Officer, and regular submission thereof to
the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month.
c) We also recommended that the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM
Officer, be supported with documentation of the
programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but not
limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment
Plan, copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization
of QRF and Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and
photographs of projects/programs/activities undertaken during
the month of report.

8.6 In view thereof, we recommended that the LCE instruct GAD Focal
Person to:
a) Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report CY 2021 based on the
format prescribed in PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-
01 and submit the same to the Auditor’s Office immediately; and
b) Henceforth, faithfully adhere to the timely submission of the GAD
AR, together with the GAD Plan and Budget, within five (5)
working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

9.6 Therefore, were recommend that the Honorable Mayor mandate:
a) The Treasurer’s Office to faithfully deposit the SET collections
in a separate depository account to prevent merging of funds
with that of the General Fund;
b) The Treasurer’s Office to faithfully prepare separate reports of
collections for the SET proceeds;
c) The Accounting/Treasurer’s Office to submit the Bank
Statements, Bank Reconciliation Statements and Deposit Slips
to substantiate the claim of creation of a separate SEF account.

10.4 We recommended and the Honorable Mayor concurred to enjoin the
Office of the Treasurer to:
a) Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled
checks; and
b) Prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month,
attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same
along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.

11.5 We recommended and Management concurred that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer shall observe the stamping of “PAID”,
with the prescribed size of 2’’ x 3’’, on all the DVs and its
supporting documents after payment is made.
b) The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment
shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents
to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in
payment of future claims.
c) The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including
its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s
Office are stamped “PAID”.

1.6 Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an
extensive inventory of the road network system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road
network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

2.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously
post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and
property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep
and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the
Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and
ARE as prescribed in support of the PPE Cards;
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an extensive annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit
the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each
year; and
d) Mandate the Accountant to religiously conform to the required
disclosure of PPE in the financial statements pursuant to IPSAS
17.

3.6 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) cause
the immediate submission of both the approved supplemental budget
and supplemental AIP to the audit team to facilitate assessment on the
validity and appropriateness of PPAs implemented.

4.5 In view of the noted deficiencies, we highly recommended that the Local
Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee:
a) To submit certified true copies of certifications of posting
requirements by the BAC Secretariat relative to the BGCM PPA
procurements;
b) To immediately submit the required monthly reports on the
BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs
pursuant to LBC 125;
c) Though late, still post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s
website or Facebook page for general transparency; and
d) Finally, furnish the audit team a copy of the written notice when
posting has been complied.

5.9 Due to the foregoing lapses noted, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of
supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same
as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the
appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards;
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis; and
d) Direct the Inventory Committee to validate the same by
conducting a semestral physical count of inventory by items.
Thereafter, prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team not later than July 31, and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively.

6.8 Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Immediately instruct the Accountant to prepare an amortization
schedule of the outstanding loan and reconcile the same with the
statement of accounts from the servicing bank and update the
related accounting ledger to reflect the correct balance;
b) Reclassify the portion of the loan not payable within the 12
months from the reporting date from current into non-current
liability;
c) Instruct the same to update and reconcile the accounting ledgers
with the statement of accounts from GSIS, Pag-IBIG, Philhealth
and BIR to reflect correct balances of related liability accounts,
and if there are discrepancies found, immediately make the
necessary adjustments; and
d) Henceforth, maintain and keep the necessary accounting ledgers
updated to make information available as of any specific date,
pursuant to Section 111 of PD 1445.

7.6 Hence, we strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of the copy of the approved
Local Sanggunian Resolution on the release and utilization of the
QRF to the audit team;
b) Continue the religious keeping, maintaining, and updating of the
three (3) RAAOs in accordance with the rules of the Commission;
c) Instruct the strict, religious and regular submission of the report
on sources and utilization not later than the 15th day after each
month;
d) Instruct the Inventory Committee and the LDRRMO to
immediately do an extensive physical count of all the equipment
under the MDRRMO to update the ledgers on new PPEs
purchased or acquired thru donation; and
e) Instruct the Accountant to draw appropriate journal entries to
record or adjust the ledgers for any discrepancies resulting from
the physical count.

8.8 Henceforth, we recommended that:
a) The Treasurer keep a permanent record book to keep track of the
receipt and issuances of accountable forms, and update the same
as needed;
b) The Treasurer conduct a regular month-end inventory of the
accountable forms, both with and without money value, to verify
existence, and reconcile the same with the permanent record
book;
c) Prepare the monthly C/RAAFs at the end of each month, complete
and in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations; and
d) Strictly submit the same to the audit team not later than the fifth
(5th) day of the ensuing month.

9.4 Therefore, we recommended that the Treasurer, henceforth:
a) Completely attach all duplicate copies of all issued, and
cancelled ORs to the RCD;
b) Attach the validated deposit slips to the RCD as proof of
deposit of collections; and
c) Prepare a schedule of cancelled/voided checks, if any, at the
end of the month, and attach the same to the RCD to be
submitted to the Auditor.

10.10 a) Record the transactions on a daily basis and stop the practice
of recording transactions based from the bank statements;
b) Record the check issuances in a chronological order,
including the cancelled ones;
c) Completely report and include in the RCD all collections
recorded in the cashbook; and
d) Finally, religiously follow and record all the transactions in
the respective cashbooks in accordance with the MNGAS for
LGUs.

11.7 Therefore, steadfast, we recommended that:
a) The Accountant faithfully inspect and review records whether the
previous cash advances have been liquidated and properly
accounted for prior to the granting of the succeeding cash
advance;
b) The Accountant, after verification that are no cash advances
pending liquidation, issue a certification thereof prior to the
granting of succeeding cash advance; and
c) The Treasurer religiously liquidate the cash advance within the
prescribed period and in accordance with the rules and
regulations provided by the Commission.
1. Create the Inventory Committee of the municipality to conduct the inventory on the physical count of its road network and prepare a report thereon;

4. In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management widen the utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects giving less priority such as social development, economic development and environment management pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
5. We recommended further that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned officials to identify in their reports which local roads are rehabilitated to avoid loss from repetition of projects.
8. Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board;
9. Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment of its constituents.

10. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the accountant to regularly maintain and update the separate registries required by the circular to ensure proper monitoring and utilization of LDRRM Fund in accordance with rules and regulations.

11. Further, to strengthen the need for the submission of the LDRRMFIP, we recommended that the said plan be regularly and strictly attached as supporting documents whenever disbursements are made hereafter, pursuant to the above stated provision to ensure that expenditures are in accordance with the approved LDRRMFIP.

12. Order the municipal treasurer the immediate transfer of the remaining unexpected balance of the fund into the LGU’s trust fund by issuing a check equal to the aforementioned outstanding unexpended balance, complete with its necessary supporting documents;
13. Likewise, order the municipal accountant to record the said transfer by recording the appropriate journal entry vouchers in its appropriate books.
14. Finally, order the accountant to fully disclose, in detail, the unexpended balance of the fund in the notes to the financial statements, hereafter, pursuant to the above cited provision.

15. We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (LDRRMF) Fund (LDDRMO) to prepare and submit Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDDRM Council.

16. We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in compliance with the above quoted rules.
19. Finally, direct the municipal accountant to immediately set up the RPT receivable account upon receipt of the list from the treasurer pursuant to the aforementioned provision.

20. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to address and comply with the deficiencies noted during the validation and submit an updated copy of the same to the Auditor for review;
21. Moreover, require the same to strictly comply with the recording, updating, and maintaining of monthly accounting records, onwards;
22. Direct the same to conscientiously keep and file copies of the all the monthly reports submitted to the auditor.

23. We, therefore, reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Honorable Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
24. We further recommended that for the CY 2020, the GAD Plan and Budget be prepared and submitted immediately to the Audit Team.

25. We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

26. We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly BRS together with the original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in bank account.

27. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time.

28. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in its prescribed form.

29. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.
30. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the Auditor.

31. We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

32. Create and Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and required the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to update their ledger cards and stock cards and make the necessary reconciliation thereof

20. Required the Treasurer shall furnish the Chief Accountant to come up with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as a basis for the one percent tax (1%) for the special education fund.

1.5 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a. Create the Inventory Committee of the municipality to conduct
the inventory on the physical count of its road network and
prepare a report thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads
network accounts based on the result of the physical count; and
c. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and
the General Services Office with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects.

2.5 In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our previous year’s
recommendation that management widen the allocation of
utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and
projects such as social development, economic development and environment
management pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC)
No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
2.6 We recommended further that the Honorable Mayor direct the
concerned officials to identify in their reports which local roads are
rehabilitated to avoid loss from repetition of projects.

3.9 Hence, we recommended that Honorable Mayor supervise the
immediate submission of duly approved Municipal Solid Waste
Management Plan 2019-2029; and
3.10 Direct the Board, if any, to review the same every two years or as the
need arises for purposes continuity and harmonizing the LGU’s
projects/programs with the mandate of RA 9003.

4.5 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the accountant to
regularly maintain and update the separate registries required by the
circular to ensure proper monitoring and utilization of LDRRM Fund in
accordance with rules and regulations.
4.6 Further, to strengthen the need for the submission of the LDRRMFIP,
we recommended that the said plan be regularly and strictly attached as
supporting documents whenever disbursements are made hereafter,
pursuant to the above stated provision to ensure that expenditures are
in accordance with the approved LDRRMFIP.

5.9 Hence, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the
Honorable Mayor, again:
a. Order the municipal treasurer the immediate transfer of the
remaining unexpected balance of the fund into the LGU’s trust
fund by issuing a check equal to the aforementioned outstanding
unexpended balance, complete with its necessary supporting
documents;
b. Likewise, order the municipal accountant to record the said
transfer by recording the appropriate journal entry vouchers in
its appropriate books.
c. Finally, order the accountant to fully disclose, in detail, the
unexpended balance of the fund in the notes to the financial
statements, hereafter, pursuant to the above cited provision.

6.4 Therefore, we highly recommended that the Mayor strictly instruct the
keeping of an updated real property tax account register to the
concerned officials;
a) Direct the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit the updated
list of duly certified name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year to the accountant at the beginning of the
year; and
b) Finally, direct the municipal accountant to immediately set up the
RPT receivable account upon receipt of the list from the
treasurer pursuant to the aforementioned provision.

7.9 Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials
to address and comply with the deficiencies noted during the
validation and submit an updated copy of the same to the
Auditor for review;
b) Moreover, require the same to strictly comply with the recording,
updating, and maintaining of monthly accounting records,
onwards;
c) Direct the same to conscientiously keep and file copies of the all
the monthly reports submitted to the auditor.

8.3 We, therefore, reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the
Honorable Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to furnish the Auditor a
copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs,
projects and activities undertaken during the year.
8.4 We further recommended that for the CY 2020, the GAD Plan and
Budget be prepared and submitted immediately to the Audit Team.

9.5 Hence, we recommended that LGU exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

11. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time.

12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in its prescribed form.

13. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the law in the preparation and submission of Reports of Collections and Deposits and its supporting documents like the Official Receipts and Validated Deposit Slips on a monthly basis.

14. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the supporting instruments in every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.

2.5 We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and
submit Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund

3.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a) The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete
supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended
LDRRM Fund in the amount of P712,000.00 from General Fund
to Special Trust Fund, and.
b) The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended
LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the
appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.

i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of
Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds
(Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in
Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-
01-02-010).
ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank
– Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-
010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-
020).

3.5 We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and
other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts
and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its
perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents
pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

4.6 We recommended that Management
a) Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and
b) Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological
Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of
public health and environment of its constituents.

5.3 We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of
the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects
and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend
that for the CY 2019, the GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared
and submitted immediately to the Audit Team.

6.8 We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant
prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly the BRS together with the
original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in
bank account.

6.9 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a
General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund
and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for
proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time.

7.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in
its prescribed form.

8.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer
to strictly comply with the provisions of the law in the preparation
and submission of Reports of Collections and Deposits and its
supporting documents like the Official Receipts and Validated Deposit
Slips on a monthly basis.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of
Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together
with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of
the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the
NGAS for LGUs.
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and
evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then
prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the
Auditor.
c) Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate
submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor and the
prompt submission thereafter.

9.8 Management was able to submit all the unsubmitted RCI together with its
disburement vouchers in March 29, 2019.

10.5 We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
